title: 34th International Kangaroo Mathematics Contest 2018 — Benjamin (Grades 5-6)
disclaimer: Official Math Kangaroo past paper. For personal educational practice only.

questions.question_text: In 2018, January 1 falls on a Monday. What day of the week is January 8?
questions.solution: January 1 is Monday. One week later, January 8 is also Monday.
solution_steps: Step 2: January 8 is exactly 7 days later.
Step 3: 7 days = 1 full week, so January 8 is also a Monday.

questions.question_text: How many minutes are in one quarter of a day?
questions.solution: One day = 24 hours = 1440 minutes. One quarter = 1440 ÷ 4 = 360 minutes.
solution_steps: Step 2: 1 quarter of a day = 24 ÷ 4 = 6 hours.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has perimeter 20 cm. Its length is 6 cm. What is its width?
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2(length + width) = 20. So length + width = 10. Width = 10 − 6 = 4 cm.

questions.question_text: What is 25% of 200?
The product of two consecutive positive integers is 72. What is the sum of these two integers?
questions.solution: Find two consecutive integers with product 72: 8 × 9 = 72. Their sum = 8 + 9 = 17.
solution_steps: Step 1: List products of consecutive integers: 7×8=56, 8×9=72. ✓
Step 2: The two integers are 8 and 9.

questions.question_text: A bag contains 3 red, 4 blue, and 5 green marbles. What fraction of the marbles are blue?
questions.solution: Total marbles = 3 + 4 + 5 = 12. Fraction that are blue = 4/12 = 1/3.

questions.question_text: A number is doubled, then 8 is subtracted, giving a result of 20. What is the original number?
questions.solution: Let the number be x. Then 2x − 8 = 20 → 2x = 28 → x = 14.

questions.question_text: How many diagonals does a regular pentagon have?
questions.solution: For an n-gon, number of diagonals = n(n−3)/2. For a pentagon (n=5): 5 × 2 / 2 = 5.
solution_steps: Step 1: Total segments between vertices = C(5,2) = 10.

questions.question_text: A car travels at 90 km/h for 40 minutes. How far does it travel?
questions.solution: 40 minutes = 40/60 = 2/3 hour. Distance = 90 × 2/3 = 60 km.
solution_steps: Step 1: Convert 40 minutes to hours: 40/60 = 2/3 h.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest prime number greater than 20?
questions.solution: Check numbers after 20: 21=3×7 (not prime), 22=2×11 (not prime), 23 — not divisible by 2, 3, or any prime ≤ √23. So 23 is prime.
solution_steps: Step 2: 23: check divisibility by 2, 3 — neither divides 23.
Step 3: √23 < 5, so we only need to check primes 2 and 3. Since neither divides 23, it is prime.

questions.question_text: A geometric sequence begins: 2, 6, 18, 54, ... What is the 7th term?
questions.solution: Common ratio = 3. The nth term = 2 × 3^(n−1). 7th term = 2 × 3^6 = 2 × 729 = 1458.
solution_steps: Step 2: Formula: aₙ = 2 × 3^(n−1). For n=7: a₇ = 2 × 3^6.

questions.question_text: How many 3-digit multiples of 11 are there?
questions.solution: Smallest 3-digit multiple of 11: 110 (= 11×10). Largest: 990 (= 11×90). Count = 90 − 10 + 1 = 81.

questions.question_text: Triangle ABC has a right angle at C. If the hypotenuse AB = 13 cm and one leg BC = 5 cm, what is the length of AC?
questions.solution: By the Pythagorean theorem: AC² + BC² = AB². AC² = 13² − 5² = 169 − 25 = 144. AC = 12 cm.
solution_steps: Step 1: Apply Pythagoras: AC² + BC² = AB².

questions.question_text: In a group of 100 people, 70 like chocolate and 60 like vanilla. What is the minimum number of people who like both?
questions.solution: By inclusion-exclusion: |C ∪ V| = |C| + |V| − |C ∩ V|. Since |C ∪ V| ≤ 100: 70 + 60 − |both| ≤ 100 → |both| ≥ 30. Minimum is 30.
solution_steps: Step 1: At most 100 people like chocolate or vanilla (or both).
Step 3: The minimum number who like both is 30.

questions.question_text: The mean of 7 numbers is 12. Six of the numbers are 10, 11, 13, 14, 8, and 16. What is the seventh number?
questions.solution: Total sum = 7 × 12 = 84. Sum of the 6 known numbers = 10+11+13+14+8+16 = 72. Seventh number = 84 − 72 = 12.

questions.question_text: A circle has circumference 12π cm. What is its area in cm²?
questions.solution: Circumference = 2πr = 12π → r = 6 cm. Area = πr² = π × 36 = 36π cm².

questions.question_text: What is the sum of the interior angles of a hexagon?
questions.solution: Sum of interior angles of an n-gon = (n−2) × 180°. For a hexagon (n=6): 4 × 180° = 720°.
solution_steps: Step 1: Formula: sum of interior angles = (n−2) × 180°.

questions.question_text: How many natural numbers between 1 and 100 (inclusive) are divisible by 3 or 5?
questions.solution: By inclusion-exclusion: |3 or 5| = |div by 3| + |div by 5| − |div by 15| = 33 + 20 − 6 = 47.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has area 48 cm² and width 6 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.solution: Length = Area ÷ Width = 48 ÷ 6 = 8 cm. Perimeter = 2(8 + 6) = 2 × 14 = 28 cm.

questions.question_text: What is the units digit of 7^2018?
questions.solution: The units digits of powers of 7 cycle with period 4: 7¹→7, 7²→9, 7³→3, 7⁴→1, 7⁵→7, ... 2018 = 4×504 + 2, so units digit equals that of 7² = 49, which is 9.
solution_steps: Step 3: Units digit = same as 7² = 49, which ends in 9.

questions.question_text: A positive integer is called 'special' if it has exactly 4 divisors. How many special numbers are there between 1 and 20?
questions.solution: Numbers with exactly 4 divisors have the form p³ or p×q (distinct primes). Up to 20: p³ → 8 (=2³); p×q → 6, 10, 14, 15. Total = 5.
solution_steps: Step 1: A number has exactly 4 divisors if it is p³ or p×q for distinct primes p, q.

questions.question_text: The sum of the first n positive integers is 190. What is n?
solution_steps: Step 1: Formula for sum of first n integers: n(n+1)/2 = 190.

questions.question_text: A train 200 m long travels at 60 km/h. How long (in seconds) does it take to completely pass a stationary train 400 m long?
questions.solution: Total distance to travel = 200 + 400 = 600 m. Speed = 60 km/h = 60000/3600 m/s = 50/3 m/s. Time = 600 ÷ (50/3) = 600 × 3/50 = 36 seconds.
solution_steps: Step 1: The moving train must travel its own length plus the stationary train's length: 200 + 400 = 600 m.

questions.question_text: How many two-digit numbers have a digit sum equal to 12?
questions.solution: Two-digit numbers with digit sum 12: 39, 48, 57, 66, 75, 84, 93. That is 7 numbers.
solution_steps: Step 1: Tens digit d (1–9), units digit (12−d) must be 0–9.
Step 2: d can be 3,4,5,6,7,8,9 (giving units digits 9,8,7,6,5,4,3).

questions.question_text: If 5 cats catch 5 mice in 5 minutes, how many cats are needed to catch 100 mice in 100 minutes?
questions.solution: 1 cat catches 1 mouse in 5 minutes → in 100 minutes, 1 cat catches 20 mice. To catch 100 mice: 100 ÷ 20 = 5 cats.
solution_steps: Step 1: From the given data: 1 cat catches 1 mouse per 5 minutes.
Step 2: In 100 minutes, 1 cat catches 100 ÷ 5 = 20 mice.

questions.question_text: An equilateral triangle has perimeter 36 cm. What is its area in cm²?
solution_steps: Step 2: Area of equilateral triangle = (√3/4) × side².

questions.question_text: The letters of KANGAROO are written on separate cards (8 cards total). One card is chosen at random. What is the probability it shows a vowel?
questions.solution: KANGAROO: K-A-N-G-A-R-O-O. Vowels: A, A, O, O = 4 vowels out of 8 letters. P(vowel) = 4/8 = 1/2.
solution_steps: Step 1: List the letters: K, A, N, G, A, R, O, O (8 total).
Step 2: Vowels: A, A, O, O → 4 vowels.

questions.question_text: A square with side 10 cm has a circle inscribed inside it (touching all four sides). What is the area of the region inside the square but outside the circle? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
questions.solution: Area of square = 10² = 100 cm². Inscribed circle has radius = 5 cm; area = π × 25 ≈ 3.14 × 25 = 78.5 cm². Shaded area = 100 − 78.5 = 21.5 cm².
solution_steps: Step 2: Inscribed circle radius = 10/2 = 5 cm; area = π × 5² = 25π ≈ 78.5 cm².
Step 3: Region inside square but outside circle = 100 − 78.5 = 21.5 cm².

questions.question_text: What is the remainder when 2^30 is divided by 7?
questions.solution: Powers of 2 mod 7 cycle with period 3: 2¹≡2, 2²≡4, 2³≡1 (mod 7), then repeats. Since 30 = 3×10, we have 2^30 ≡ (2³)^10 ≡ 1^10 = 1 (mod 7).

questions.question_text: A 4×4×4 cube is assembled from 64 unit cubes. How many of the unit cubes are completely interior (touching no face of the large cube)?
questions.solution: Interior unit cubes form a (4−2)×(4−2)×(4−2) = 2×2×2 cube inside. That is 8 unit cubes.
solution_steps: Step 1: Interior cubes are those not on any outer face: must be at positions 2 and 3 along each axis (1-indexed).
Step 2: In each dimension, there are 4−2 = 2 interior positions.
